title: toxicodendron diversilobum
intro: Toxicodendron diversilobum, also known as poison oak, is a plant species native to the western United States and Canada, characterized by its compound leaves that cause severe skin irritation in many people upon contact. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which can be utilized to treat various health issues. The health benefits of the plant include its ability to alleviate symptoms of eczema, acne, and other skin conditions, as well as its potential to reduce fever and relieve pain. Herbal preparations of Toxicodendron diversilobum can be made into salves, ointments, and tinctures, and are often used topically to soothe and calm irritated skin.
